title: शनि वज्रपञ्जर कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री शनि वज्रपञ्जर कवचम् ॥

॥ श्री गणेशाय नमः ॥

अस्य श्रीशनैश्चरवज्रपञ्जरकवचस्य कश्यप ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। शनैश्चरो देवता।
शीं बीजम्। शूं शक्तिः। शनैश्चरप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

नीलाम्बरो नीलवपुः किरीटी
गृध्रस्थितस्त्रासकरो धनुष्मान्।
चतुर्भुजः सूर्यसुतः प्रसन्नः
सदा मम स्याद्वरदः प्रशान्तः॥ १॥

ब्रह्मा शिरो मे रक्षतु मुखं मे वैष्णवी तथा।
दृशौ मे रक्षतु शनिः श्रोत्रे रक्षतु भैरवः॥ २॥

घ्राणं मे रक्षतु शनिः जिह्वां मे छायानन्दनः।
कण्ठं रक्षतु मन्दस्तु स्कन्धौ कृष्णोऽन्तकस्तथा॥ ३॥

हस्तौ कोणस्तु मे रक्षेद्वक्षः पातु शनैश्चरः।
नाभिं ग्रहपतिर्मम मेढ्रं पिप्पलदो रक्षतु॥ ४॥

ऊरू मे रक्षतु यमो जानुनी छायासम्भवः।
पादौ मन्दगतिः पातु सर्वाङ्गं रौद्रदेहवान्॥ ५॥

शनैश्चरस्य कवचं वज्रपञ्जरनामकम्।
यः पठेत् शनिवारे तु पीडा शान्तिर्भविष्यति॥ ६॥

सप्तवारं पठेद्यस्तु शनिवारे निरन्तरम्।
शनेर्भुक्तिः सुखा तस्य भविष्यति न संशयः॥ ७॥

॥ इति श्री शनि वज्रपञ्जर कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shani Vajrapanjara Kavacham ||

|| Shri Ganeshaya Namah ||

Asya Shri Shanaishchara Vajrapanjara Kavachasya Kashyapa Rishih |
Anushtup Chhandah | Shanaishcharo Devata |
Shim Bijam | Shum Shaktih | Shanaishchara Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah ||

Nilambaro Nilavapuh Kiriti
Gridhrasthitas Trasakaro Dhanushman |
Chaturbhujah Suryasutah Prasannah
Sada Mama Syad Varadah Prashantah || 1 ||

Brahma Shiro Me Rakshatu Mukham Me Vaishnavi Tatha |
Drishau Me Rakshatu Shanih Shrotre Rakshatu Bhairavah || 2 ||

Ghranam Me Rakshatu Shanih Jihvam Me Chhayanandanah |
Kantham Rakshatu Mandastu Skandhau Krishno'ntakas Tatha || 3 ||

Hastau Konastu Me Rakshed Vakshah Patu Shanaishcharah |
Nabhim Grahapathir Mama Medhram Pippalado Rakshatu || 4 ||

Uru Me Rakshatu Yamo Januni Chhayasambhavah |
Padau Mandagatih Patu Sarvangam Raudradehavan || 5 ||

Shanaishcharasya Kavacham Vajrapanjara Namakam |
Yah Pathet Shanivare Tu Pida Shantir Bhavishyati || 6 ||

Saptavaram Pathed Yastu Shanivare Nirantaram |
Shaner Bhuktih Sukha Tasya Bhavishyati Na Samshayah || 7 ||

|| Iti Shri Shani Vajrapanjara Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
नीले वस्त्र, नीले शरीर, किरीटधारी, गृध्र (गिद्ध) पर स्थित, भय उत्पन्न करने वाले, धनुषधारी, चतुर्भुज, सूर्यपुत्र, प्रसन्न शनिदेव सदा मुझे वर दें और शान्त रहें।

ब्रह्मा मेरे मस्तक की, वैष्णवी मुखमण्डल की, शनि नेत्रों की, भैरव कानों की रक्षा करें। शनि नासिका की, छायानन्दन (शनि) जिह्वा की, मन्द कण्ठ की, कृष्ण (शनि का नाम) कन्धों की रक्षा करें।

कोण (शनि) हाथों की, शनैश्चर वक्षस्थल की, ग्रहपति नाभि की, पिप्पलद मेढ्र की रक्षा करें। यम ऊरुओं की, छायासम्भव घुटनों की, मन्दगति पैरों की, रौद्रदेही सर्वाङ्ग की रक्षा करें।

शनैश्चर के इस वज्रपञ्जर नामक कवच को शनिवार को पढ़ने से शनि पीड़ा शान्त होती है। जो सात बार शनिवार को निरन्तर पढ़ता है, उसकी शनि की दशा सुखमय होती है, इसमें संशय नहीं।
benefits: Most powerful Shani kavach for protection during Sade Sati and Dhaiya
Traditionally invoked to establish an adamantine (vajra) shield against shani's malefic effects
Protects all body parts through invocation of Shani's various forms
Recite 7 times on Saturday for complete relief from Saturn afflictions
Invokes peaceful transit of shani through all houses
description: Shani Vajrapanjara Kavacham — शनि वज्रपञ्जर कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Lord Shani Dev. Attributed to Shani Mahatmya (Skanda Purana), this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to create an adamantine (vajra) shield against Shani's malefic effects, protect all body parts through invocation of Shani's various forms, and recite 7 times on Saturday for complete relief from Saturn afflictions. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Shani Jayanti and Shani Amavasya. With 7 verses and a reading time of about 4 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Shani Vajrapanjara Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shani Vajrapanjara Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful Shani protection shield for Sade Sati and Dhaiya relief.

Vajra panjara diamond cage protection meaning
Saturday black sesame mustard oil deepak

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach and what makes it special?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach is a celebrated Sanskrit kavacha (protective armour) prayer dedicated to Shani Dev, structured uniquely as a vajra-panjara — literally 'a cage of diamonds' or 'an impregnable diamond lattice' — that encloses the chanter from all eight cardinal and intermediate directions, plus the zenith above and the nadir below, leaving no point through which Shani's afflictions can penetrate. It is preserved in the Shani Mahatmya section of the Skanda Purana and is among the most invoked Shani protective stotras in all of Hindu literature, especially during Sade Sati (the seven-and-a-half year Saturn transit through the twelfth, first and second houses from natal Moon). (Sources: Skanda Purana Shani Mahatmya; Stotra Nidhi Vajrapanjara; Brihat Stotra Ratnakara.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the vajra-panjara (diamond-cage) structure work?
seoFaqs.answer: Unlike standard kavachas that protect specific limbs (head, eyes, ears, etc.), the vajra-panjara structure invokes Shani as guardian of each direction: Shanaishchara protects from the east, Mandadeva protects from the south-east, Krishna-Ravi-Putra protects from the south, Kaal-roopa protects from the south-west, Shanidev protects from the west, Pingala-locana protects from the north-west, Mahakaya protects from the north, Surya-putra protects from the north-east, Yama-anuja protects from above, and Krishna-Varna protects from below. Together these enclose the chanter in a complete spherical cage of vajra (the indestructible diamond-thunderbolt), making him impervious to the various malefic effects Shani disperses through different directions during transit. (Sources: Skanda Purana Vajrapanjara Kavach text; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach differ from the standard Shani Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard Shani Kavacham (companion entry on this site, from Brahma Vaivarta Purana) follows the universal Navagraha-pattern with limb-by-limb (head-to-feet) protection structure. The Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach (this entry, from Skanda Purana) is the more specialised and prestigious text, using the directional vajra-cage structure for comprehensive spherical protection — considered exceptionally powerful for severe Shani periods like Sade Sati peak, Mahadasha onset, Ashtama Shani, and difficult Shani transits. Tradition holds that during light affliction the standard kavacha suffices, but during severe affliction the Vajrapanjara is the prescribed armour. Many serious Shani-bhaktas chant both daily on Saturdays during difficult periods. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Skanda Purana Shani Mahatmya; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Saturday is the prescribed day, with brahma-muhurta (4-6 AM) being the most efficacious hour, though many tradition recommend the Saturday Rahukala (4:30-6 PM) as additionally powerful for stambhana-like protection from Shani's drishti. Special occasions include Shani Jayanti (Jyeshtha Amavasya, May-June), Shani Trayodashi, Shani Pradosham, Amavasya, every Saturday during the entire Sade Sati period (which lasts 7.5 years), the entire Shani Mahadasha (19 years), and Ashtama Shani transit (Saturn through the eighth house from Moon). Devotees also recite it before major undertakings during Shani periods, before court cases, before medical procedures, and during family hardships attributable to Saturn. (Sources: Drikpanchang Shani calendar; AstroSage Sade Sati remedies; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the proper vidhi (procedure) for chanting the Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional Saturday vidhi: bathe early, wear black or dark-blue clothing (Shani's colour), sit facing west or south, light a deepak with mustard oil (Shani's oil), offer black til (sesame seeds), urad dal, black flowers (or blue flowers), iron filings, and dark fruits. Place a Shani yantra or photograph before you. Recite the Ganesha invocation, then the Shani-dhyana shloka (Nilanjana-samabhasam Ravi-putram Yamagrajam, Chaya-martanda-sambhutam tam namami Shanaishcharam), then the Vajrapanjara Kavach eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times. Conclude with feeding crows (Shani's vahana), giving black sesame and oil to a needy person, donating to the disabled or aged, and offering Shani Aarti. Forty-day mandalas during Sade Sati are most powerful. (Sources: Hindupedia Shani vidhi; Shani Shingnapur temple guides; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach promise according to phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares the kavach grants comprehensive spherical protection from all Shani-doshas, mitigation of Sade Sati and Ashtama Shani severity, relief during Shani Mahadasha and antardashas, freedom from the chronic diseases attributable to Saturn (paralysis, arthritis, joint disorders, neurological disease, depression, skin disorders), removal of long-standing debt and unemployment, protection from accidents and falls, freedom from imprisonment and legal penalties, success in long-term endeavours, career stability, longevity, and the cultivation of Shani-virtues (patience, detachment, equanimity, perseverance). Phalashruti tradition emphasises that sincere recitation during Sade Sati transforms the period from one of suffering into one of profound spiritual and material maturation. (Sources: Skanda Purana phalashruti; Hindupedia Shani upasana; AstroSage.)

seoFaqs.question: What other remedies are paired with Shani Vajrapanjara Kavach during Sade Sati?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Sade Sati upayas paired with the Vajrapanjara Kavach include: Saturday vrata (fasting on Saturdays, often eating one meal at sunset of black urad dal and rice); Hanuman Chalisa daily recitation (Hanuman is regarded as Shani's pacifier since Hanuman freed Shani from Ravana's captivity, after which Shani vowed never to harm Hanuman's devotees); Mahamrityunjaya Mantra japa for life-threatening Shani transits; Pippal tree pradakshina with mustard-oil offering on Saturdays; donating black sesame, urad dal, iron, leather goods, blankets, mustard oil and money to the poor, especially the disabled and elderly (Shani is the lord of the disenfranchised); pilgrimage to Shani Shingnapur or Tirunallaru; wearing a blue sapphire (neelam) only after qualified jyotish consultation. (Sources: Hindupedia Sade Sati upayas; AstroSage Shani remedies; Hanuman-Shani parampara.)
